title: [InstCombine] Stop propagating `undef` when element is demanded
summary: The generalization centers on recognizing that `PoisonValue` (replacing `UndefValue`) can safely propagate through demanded element analysis, enabling broader pattern matching. The patch changes a single `isa<UndefValue>` check to `isa<PoisonValue>`, allowing the transform to handle more cases where poison semantics apply—such as multiple shuffle uses, blends with loads, and cross-type conversions—without being blocked by conservative undef handling. This small semantic shift unlocks optimizations across diverse shuffle patterns, demonstrating that updating legacy undef checks to poison can systematically expand transformation coverage. For similar optimizations, identify where undef/poison distinctions unnecessarily restrict pattern matching and update those predicates to use poison where safe.
